title: Groundwater Contaminant Transport Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Groundwater Contaminant Transport Inversion: Groundwater contaminant transport inversion: infer hydraulic conductivity K field from head and concentration data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.darcy.groundwater_flow…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~2000; density_dependent_flow_effects, preferential_flow_paths set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: nabla*(K*nabla h) = S_s*(dh/dt) + q (Darcy); dC/dt + v*nabla C = nabla*(D*nabla C) - lambda*C
E.world_state_x: hydraulic conductivity field K(x,y,z) in 3D aquifer domain
E.observation_y: hydraulic head h(t) at N_wells observation wells, concentration breakthrough curves

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_hydraulic_conductivity is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 2000); density_dependent_flow_effects dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Measurement gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: head RMSE < 0.1 m; concentration within factor 2. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~2000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |density_dependent_flow_effects|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_ensemble * N_sim * N_iter) or O(N_pilot_points ** 2 * N_obs) for PEST per iteration
